location: Dropped by Needle Knight Leda after the mandatory 'Leda and Allies' gauntlet fight, encountered on the ascent through Enir-Ilim just past the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom, shortly before the final Divine Gate boss room.
tactics: Reach the Cleansing Chamber Anteroom grace near the top of Enir-Ilim and continue up the stairs to trigger the fight automatically - it cannot be skipped on the path to the final boss.

Leda's 'allies' arrive one at a time as NPC-style invaders - Redmane Freyja, then Hornsent, then Moore, then Dryleaf Dane - before Leda herself joins last, so it's a war of attrition on stamina and FP rather than one duel.
You can place summon signs for Ansbach and Thiollier before starting to help thin out the earlier waves.

Don't confuse this with the two earlier optional Leda invasions (in Shadow Keep, and later near Ansbach's questline) - those are skippable side fights; only this final Enir-Ilim gauntlet drops the sword.
lore: This gleaming, gold-inlaid greatsword belonged to Leda, leader of the Needle Knights, and deals holy damage alongside physical. Its mirror polish can't fully hide what it implies: Leda used it to cut down her own knightly comrades during Miquella's purge of dissenters.

builds.name: Dex/Faith Needle Knight
builds.why: Scales C in Dexterity and D in Faith with only E in Strength, so a dex-faith build gets the most attack power per point invested, and the holy portion of its damage benefits from the Faith scaling too.

builds.name: Anti-buff disruptor
builds.why: Its innate Needle Piercer skill fires ten needles that strip buffs and status effects from anyone hit, making it a niche pick for invasions or fights against buff-stacking enemies and players.
